name: White Oak
summary: The generational anchor. Grows slowly but lives for centuries. White oak acorns are the least bitter and most nutritious of any eastern oak — preferred wildlife food. Deep burgundy fall color.
notes: Plant for your grandchildren's grandchildren. Matures into a broad, majestic crown. Best on well-drained upland sites.

planting.light: Full sun to partial shade (needs canopy gap)
planting.soil: Prefers deep, well-drained loam; tolerates rocky upland
planting.site_notes: Give premium spots — this tree will outlive everything else you plant. Slow starter but centuries-long payoff. Best on productive upland soils with good drainage.
